zh.lifeSummary: 當George Reighley於1816年1月6日出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州印第安納布盧申 瓦利 堂史普布盧申 瓦利時，他的父親George J. Reichley24歲，母親Magdalene Rayl23歲。他於1841年5月13日與Christena Routson在美國俄亥俄州韋恩結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1855年住在美國威斯康辛州卡盧梅特且於1880年住在美國。他去世於1895年10月2日在美國威斯康辛州卡盧梅特爾頓享年79歲，埋葬在美國威斯康辛州卡盧梅特爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Reighley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Yorkshire. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Cyhha + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Americanized form of German Kiechle (see Kuechle ) or Kiechler (see Kuechler ).

variants.name: Keithley
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): variant of   Keighley  .

variants.name: Kuechle
meanings: South German (Küchle): metonymic occupational name for a pastry cook, from Küchle ‘cookie, little cake’ (see Kuchler 1).

variants.name: Keightley
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Gatlaoich, see Gately .

variants.name: Keatley
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Keighley  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

American settlers began mining the Wisconsin Territory in the early 1800's. The lead ore in the territory had largely been mined previously by American Indians. By 1829, nearly 4,000 miners had moved to Wisconsin Territory. The miners became known as badgers as they burrowed into hillsides for shelter. The name eventually represented the state and Wisconsin is now known as the Badger State. (Wisconsin Historical Society: Lead Mining in Southwestern Wisconsin)
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: The Creation of the Republican Party
historicEvents.description: A debate continues over the location of the creation of the Republican Party. Some sources claim that the party was formed in Ripon, Wisconsin, on February 28, 1854. Others claim the first meeting of the Republican Party took place in Jackson, Michigan, on July 6, 1854, where the Republican Party was officially organized. Over 1,000 people were present and candidates were selected for the party, thus making it the first Republican convention.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The three day Battle of Gettysburg was one of the bloodiest of the American Civil War. Between the Confederates and Unions, somewhere between 46,000 and 51,000 people died that day.

historicEvents.title: First National Strike in U.S. Begins In Pittsburgh Against Pennsylvania Railroad
historicEvents.description: Coming out of an economic crisis, everyone was worried when cuts started happening in the railroad. They went on what would the great railroad strike of 1877.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

en.lifeSummary: When George Reighley was born on 6 January 1816, in Brush Valley, Brush Valley Township, Indiana, Pennsylvania, United States, his father, George J. Reichley, was 24 and his mother, Magdalene Rayl, was 23. He married Christena Routson on 13 May 1841, in Wayne, Ohio, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Calumet, Wisconsin, United States in 1855 and United States in 1880. He died on 2 October 1895, in Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, United States, at the age of 79, and was buried in Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, United States.

Il a vécu à Calumet, Wisconsin, États-Unis en 1855 et à États-Unis en 1880. Il est décédé le 2 octobre 1895, à Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, États-Unis, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: George Reighleyが1816年1月6日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州インディアナブルッシュ ヴァリー トウンシュイップブルッシュ ヴァリーで生まれた時，父George J. Reichleyは24歳，母Magdalene Raylは23歳であった。1841年5月13日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州ワインでChristena Routsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1855年にはアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州カルメットに，1880年にはアメリカ合衆国に住んでいた。彼は1895年10月2日にアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州カルメットチルトンで79歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州カルメットチルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.Norton, Summit, Ohio, United States: 미국 오하이오 주 수미 노르턴 타운시프
ko.Brush Valley, Center, Pennsylvania: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 인디아나 브루시 발리 타운시프 브루시 발리
ko.lifeSummary: George Reighley(이)가 1816년 1월 6일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 인디아나 브루시 발리 타운시프 브루시 발리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George J. Reichley(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Magdalene Rayl(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1841년 5월 13일 미국 오하이오 주 웨이인에서 Christena Routson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1855년도에 미국 위스콘신 주 캘럼트에서, 1880년도에 미국에서 살았다. 그는 1895년 10월 2일 미국 위스콘신 주 캘럼트 치턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 위스콘신 주 캘럼트 치턴에 묻혔다.

ru.Norton, Summit, Ohio, United States: нортон тоуншип, суммит, Огайо, Соединенные штаты
ru.Brush Valley, Center, Pennsylvania: бруш валли, бруш валли тоуншип, индана, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Reighley родился 6 января 1816 г. в местности бруш валли, бруш валли тоуншип, индана, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени George J. Reichley было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Magdalene Rayl, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Christena Routson 13 мая 1841 г. в местности веин, Огайо, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности калумет, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты в 1855 году и в местности Соединенные штаты в 1880 году. Он умер 2 октября 1895 г. в местности чилтон, калумет, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности чилтон, калумет, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты.

es.06 Jan 1816–2 October 1895: 6 de enero de 1816–2 de octubre de 1895
es.Norton, Summit, Ohio, United States: Norton Township, Summit, Ohio, Estados Unidos
es.Brush Valley, Center, Pennsylvania: Brush Valley, Brush Valley Township, Indiana, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Reighley nació el 6 de enero de 1816, en Brush Valley, Brush Valley Township, Indiana, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, su papá, George J. Reichley, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Magdalene Rayl, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Christena Routson el 13 de mayo de 1841, en Wayne, Ohio, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Calumet, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos en 1855 y en Estados Unidos en 1880. Murió el 2 de octubre de 1895, en Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultado en Chilton, Calumet, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos.
